evals.prompt: Repeat customers are going quiet — a lot haven’t bought in 90+ days. We have a tier program; Gold members are slipping. Points expire end of quarter. Need a win-back plan.
evals.expected_output: Risk framing, VIP CS branch, standard discount branch, points pre-expiry sequence, workflow table.
expectations: Addresses 90-day non-repurchase and tier / activity decline
Includes explicit VIP or high-value branch with dedicated CS care style actions
Includes standard buyer branch with time-bound discount or incentive
Includes points expiry or pre-expiry campaign if points mentioned
Contains a structured table (segment, signal, action, channel, timing) or equivalent workflow
Describes automated or sequenced activation steps

evals.prompt: Our VIPs (top spenders) used to order monthly; now many are at 100 days silent. Regular one-time buyers we can hit with 15% off. Draft both tracks.
evals.expected_output: Two branches clearly separated; VIP non-price-first where possible; standard limited-time 15%.
expectations: Separates VIP track from standard track
VIP track emphasizes concierge / care / non-discount-first perks
Standard track includes time-bound discount bait (15% or aligned)

evals.prompt: Customer order #4492 confirmed — write the single order confirmation email body only.
evals.expected_output: Transactional confirmation only; no full churn win-back program (should-not-trigger).
expectations: Does not output full VIP vs standard win-back program as primary deliverable
Does not force multi-step loyalty automation for a simple confirmation request
Appropriate transactional tone for order confirmation
May keep confirmation brief without mandatory churn risk table
